check: GBP fully claimed and verified
description: Google Business Profile claimed verified and owned by the business not an agency-only login
why_it_matters: An unverified or agency-locked GBP is the single biggest local-visibility blocker and a common client handoff failure

check: Primary and secondary categories set correctly
description: Primary category matches the core service exactly with secondary categories covering real additional services not keyword stuffing
why_it_matters: Category selection is one of the strongest Local Pack ranking factors and is frequently set wrong or too broad

check: GBP posts and Q&A actively maintained
description: Weekly posts and proactively answered Q&A section rather than an abandoned profile
why_it_matters: Active profiles signal legitimacy to Google and give control over the Q&A narrative before competitors or spam fill it

check: Name Address Phone identical across site and all listings
description: Exact-match NAP (formatting, abbreviations, suite numbers) across the website, GBP, and every directory listing
why_it_matters: Inconsistent NAP is a direct trust signal failure that measurably suppresses local ranking

check: NAP present in crawlable HTML not just an image or embedded map
description: Address and phone number exist as real text in the page footer or contact page not only inside a Google Maps embed
why_it_matters: Search engines cannot extract NAP signal from an image or an iframe-only map

check: Core data aggregators and top industry directories listed
description: Business listed accurately on major data aggregators and the directories that matter for the specific vertical
why_it_matters: Aggregator errors propagate to dozens of downstream directories and are expensive to fix retroactively

check: Duplicate or outdated listings suppressed
description: Old addresses duplicate GBP profiles and stale directory entries identified and merged or removed
why_it_matters: Duplicate listings split review count and ranking signal and can trigger GBP suspensions

check: LocalBusiness or vertical-specific subtype schema implemented
description: JSON-LD LocalBusiness schema (or the correct subtype like Restaurant/MedicalClinic/LegalService) present with name address geo hours and price range
why_it_matters: Generic or missing local schema forfeits rich-result eligibility that competitors with correct schema capture

check: Schema NAP matches visible page and GBP exactly
description: LocalBusiness schema address and phone fields match the visible page content and GBP listing exactly with no drift
why_it_matters: Mismatched schema violates guidelines and is a documented trust signal Google actively checks

check: Active review generation process in place
description: A repeatable process (post-service email/SMS request) generating a steady flow of new reviews rather than a stagnant review count
why_it_matters: Review recency and velocity are ranking factors independent of total review count

check: All reviews responded to including negative ones
description: Owner responses present on both positive and negative reviews within a reasonable window
why_it_matters: Response rate and quality are a visible trust signal to both users and Google's ranking systems

check: Review signals monitored across platforms not just Google
description: Yelp industry-specific platforms and Facebook reviews tracked alongside GBP not treated as Google-only
why_it_matters: Multi-platform review signal feeds local pack and AI-search citability beyond Google's own index

check: Unique substantive content per location not a template swap
description: Each location page has genuinely unique content (local team unique photos specific service notes) not just city-name find-and-replace
why_it_matters: Near-duplicate location pages at scale are a well-documented thin-content penalty risk

check: Location page URL and title include city/area naturally
description: URL slug and title tag include the location in a natural non-stuffed way matching how users actually search
why_it_matters: Location-qualified queries are a large share of local search volume and need an explicit on-page match

check: Embedded map and driving directions present per location
description: Each location page includes an accurate embedded map and clear directions not just a text address
why_it_matters: Directly supports both user conversion and the crawlable-NAP requirement (SEO097) for that specific location

check: Service-area business setting configured correctly on GBP
description: Businesses without a public storefront use the service-area setting with a hidden address rather than a fake or shared storefront address
why_it_matters: Fake storefront addresses violate GBP guidelines and risk suspension which erases all accumulated local signal

check: Locally relevant content beyond service pages
description: Content addressing local context (neighborhood guides, local events, area-specific FAQs) beyond core service/location pages
why_it_matters: Local topical depth is a differentiator search engines and AI answer engines use beyond pure NAP/citation signals

check: Local backlinks from genuinely relevant community sources
description: Links from local news chambers of commerce sponsorships or industry associations, not generic low-relevance directories
why_it_matters: Locally relevant links carry disproportionate local ranking weight compared to generic link volume

check: Centralized NAP/citation management for multi-location businesses
description: A single source of truth (spreadsheet or listing-management tool) driving NAP across all locations, not each location managed ad hoc
why_it_matters: Ad hoc per-location management is how NAP drift (SEO096) accumulates at scale and becomes expensive to unwind

check: Individual GBP and location page per physical location
description: Every physical location has its own GBP profile and its own location page, never one profile representing multiple addresses
why_it_matters: One GBP covering multiple locations violates guidelines and caps visibility to a single physical point
